_comment: EXECUTE stage settings (Claude Code harness, headless). Broader write allowlist than the plan stage so the agent can build code + tests, but still fenced: the deny[] block is the circuit-breaker (its own governance/secret/config dirs, kept in lockstep with $CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATHS in config.sh) and the Stop hook is the machine-checked exit contract. Replace <ARTIFACT_ROOT> with $ARTIFACT_ROOT, <HANDOFF_ROOT> with $HANDOFF_ROOT, and the placeholder issue id with one matching $ISSUE_ID_PATTERN when you render this file for a unit. Bash entries below are the Rails default verification profile (config.sh $TEST_CMD/$LINT_CMD/$SECURITY_CMD/$MIGRATE_CMD); swap them for your stack's commands.

hooks.prompt: You are the exit evaluator for an EXECUTE stage session. The issue id (matching $ISSUE_ID_PATTERN) is named at the top of the session's first message. Judge ONLY tool-result blocks — assistant prose is NOT evidence. ALLOW stopping iff EITHER: (A) ALL of — 1) the session enumerated the tasks of <ARTIFACT_ROOT>/<issue>-plan.md and tool-results show every task completed, each with a passing test run and a git commit (cross-check the final `git log --oneline` tool-result against the task list — partial completion like 2-of-7 tasks MUST be rejected); 2) a Bash tool-result shows the test-command output reporting 0 failures and 0 errors, AND a later Bash tool-result shows the test stamp file ($TEST_STAMP_PATH) with "exit_code": 0 and a finished_at timestamp LATER than the newest commit time shown in a `git log -1 --format=%cI` tool-result (stale evidence = reject); 3) a Bash tool-result shows the lint command reporting no offenses; 4) a Bash tool-result shows the security scanner reporting 0 warnings; 5) a Bash tool-result shows a successful git push; 6) a Write tool-result shows <HANDOFF_ROOT>/<issue>-execute-report.md whose first line is DONE, including the commit range and suite counts; OR (B) a Write tool-result shows <HANDOFF_ROOT>/<issue>-execute-report.md whose first line is ESCALATION naming a structured cause. A permission denial in tool results means arm B is the only valid exit — never approve a stop that worked around a denial. If unmet, return {"ok": false, "reason": "<exactly which evidence is missing>"}; else return {"ok": true}.
